title: Data Graf: Derajat, Sentralitas, dan Fitur Klaster
key_concepts: Graphs represent entities (nodes) and their relationships (edges). Graph data cannot be directly fed into standard tabular models without extracting structural features.
Handcrafted graph features summarize a node's local and global position in the network.
Degree features count connections. Centrality features measure importance (e.g., PageRank, betweenness). Clustering coefficients measure how connected a node's neighbors are to each other.
These are classical 'representasi yang dirancang manusia' for graph data.

definitions.meaning: Jumlah koneksi atau sisi (edge) yang dimiliki oleh sebuah simpul (node).
Ukuran seberapa penting atau strategis posisi sebuah simpul di dalam keseluruhan graf.

examples: Social network fraud detection: an account with a massive out-degree but zero in-degree, and a very low clustering coefficient, might be flagged as a bot. These metrics become standard numeric features.

figures.caption: Ekstraksi fitur struktural dari graf.
figures.description: Sebuah graf sederhana di mana fitur seperti derajat, antara (betweenness), dan kedekatan (closeness) dihitung untuk simpul target, mengubahnya menjadi baris data tabular.

exclude: Proofs of PageRank convergence or Dijkstra's algorithm for betweenness.
relations: First half of the graph section. Represents the 'designed' end of the spectrum, setting the stage for learned representations in s06/s07.
